name: Tomato
info: A widely grown garden vegetable, known for its juicy fruit used in salads and sauces. They are rich in lycopene and vitamin C.
howToPlant: Sow seeds 1/4 inch deep in seed-starting mix. Maintain temperature between 70-80°F. Transplant outdoors after last frost in a sunny spot with stakes or cages for support. Deep watering at the base is essential.
diseases: Early Blight, Late Blight, Blossom End Rot, Fusarium Wilt
prevention: Rotate crops every 3 years, ensure calcium-rich soil, mulch to keep soil moisture even, and avoid overhead watering to prevent fungal issues.

name: Carrot
info: A root vegetable usually orange, though purple, black, red, white, and yellow cultivars exist. They are highly nutritious and store well.
howToPlant: Sow seeds directly into deep, loose, stone-free soil 3 weeks before last frost. Keep soil consistently moist during germination, which can take up to 3 weeks. Thin seedlings to 2-3 inches apart.
diseases: Leaf Blight, Carrot Root Fly, Cavity Spot
prevention: Cover with fine mesh to exclude flies, thin seedlings properly to avoid overcrowding, and avoid over-fertilizing with nitrogen which causes forking.

name: Potato
info: A starchy tuberous crop from the perennial nightshade Solanum tuberosum. It is one of the world's most important food crops.
howToPlant: Plant seed potatoes 4 inches deep in loose, fertile soil. Space 12 inches apart. Hill soil around stems as they grow to prevent tubers from turning green.
diseases: Early Blight, Late Blight, Common Scab, Colorado Potato Beetle
prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, hill plants diligently, rotate with non-nightshades, and pick off beetles by hand.

name: Onion
info: Hardy bulbs used as a base for many culinary dishes worldwide. They come in short-day, long-day, and day-neutral varieties.
howToPlant: Plant sets 1 inch deep in early spring. Prefers full sun and nitrogen-rich soil. Ensure soil is well-drained to prevent bulb rot.
diseases: Downy Mildew, Onion Fly, Neck Rot, Thrips
prevention: Improve drainage, rotate crops annually, dry bulbs thoroughly after harvest, and avoid high nitrogen late in the season.

name: Garlic
info: A bulbous flowering plant in the genus Allium, closely related to the onion.
howToPlant: Plant individual cloves root-side down in fall, 4-6 inches apart in well-drained soil.
prevention: Do not plant in infested soil, use clean stock, and ensure good air circulation.

name: Broccoli
info: An edible green plant in the cabbage family whose large flowering head is eaten as a vegetable.
howToPlant: Plant seeds or transplants in spring/fall. Needs cool weather and consistent moisture.
prevention: Maintain soil pH above 6.8, rotate crops, and use floating row covers.

name: Spinach
info: A leafy green flowering plant native to central and western Asia.
howToPlant: Sow seeds 1/2 inch deep. Prefers cool weather; plant in early spring or late fall.
prevention: Avoid overcrowding, provide good drainage, and use resistant varieties.

name: Lettuce
info: An annual plant of the daisy family, Asteraceae, most commonly grown as a leaf vegetable.
howToPlant: Sow seeds shallowly as they need light to germinate. Successive plantings every 2 weeks.
prevention: Improve airflow, remove old leaves, and use beer traps for slugs.

name: Bell Pepper
info: Sweet peppers that come in various colors including red, yellow, orange, and green.
howToPlant: Start seeds indoors 8 weeks before frost. Needs warm soil and bright sun.
prevention: Mulch plants, avoid handling when wet, and ensure adequate foliage cover.

name: Cucumber
info: A widely-cultivated creeping vine plant that bears cylindrical fruits used as vegetables.
howToPlant: Sow seeds directly in mounds after soil warms. Provide a trellis for climbing varieties.
prevention: Use resistant cultivars, control Cucumber Beetles, and avoid overhead watering.

name: Zucchini
info: A fast-growing summer squash that can reach significant sizes if left unharvested.
howToPlant: Plant seeds directly in rich soil after all frost. Space 2-3 feet apart.
prevention: Cover stems with soil, use row covers until flowers appear, and ensure air circulation.

name: Eggplant
info: A species of nightshade grown for its edible purple fruit.
howToPlant: Start indoors 8-10 weeks before last frost. Needs very warm temperatures to thrive.
prevention: Avoid planting where other nightshades grew, use row covers for beetles.

name: Radish
info: An edible root vegetable of the family Brassicaceae that was domesticated in Asia.
howToPlant: Sow seeds directly 1/2 inch deep. Quickest vegetable to harvest.
prevention: Rotate crops, apply wood ash, and maintain even soil moisture.

name: Beetroot
info: The taproot portion of a beet plant, usually known in North America as beets.
howToPlant: Sow seeds 1/2 inch deep, 1 inch apart. Soak seeds overnight for better germination.
prevention: Remove infected leaves, ensure good drainage, and maintain neutral soil pH.

name: Cabbage
info: A leafy green, red, or white biennial plant grown as an annual vegetable for its dense heads.
howToPlant: Plant in early spring/late summer. Needs heavy feeding with compost/fertilizer.
prevention: Clean up garden debris, use BT for worms, and choose resistant varieties.

name: Cauliflower
info: One of several vegetables in the species Brassica oleracea, in the family Brassicaceae.
howToPlant: Needs consistent temperature and moisture. Blanch heads by tying leaves over them.
prevention: Avoid waterlogging, maintain high pH, and use disease-free transplants.

name: Peas
info: The small spherical seed or the seed-pod of the pod fruit Pisum sativum.
howToPlant: Sow as soon as soil can be worked in spring. Provide support for climbing types.
prevention: Use well-drained soil, avoid walking on wet ground, and plant early.

name: Green Beans
info: The unripe, young fruit and protective pods of various cultivars of the common bean.
howToPlant: Plant seeds directly when soil is warm. Bush varieties don't need support; pole beans do.
prevention: Avoid working in the garden when leaves are wet, rotate crops.

name: Kale
info: A cabbage in which the central leaves do not form a head.
howToPlant: Extremely hardy; can grow well into winter. Best flavor after a light frost.
prevention: Provide space for air movement, avoid overhead watering, remove diseased leaves.

name: Asparagus
info: A perennial flowering plant species in the genus Asparagus.
howToPlant: Plant crowns in deep trenches. Do not harvest for the first 2-3 years to establish roots.
prevention: Burn old stalks in winter, use resistant 'Jersey' varieties.

name: Celery
info: A marshland plant in the family Apiaceae that has been cultivated as a vegetable since antiquity.
howToPlant: Needs a long, cool growing season and lots of water and nutrients.
prevention: Control leafhoppers (which spread virus), avoid nitrogen excess.

name: Pumpkin
info: A cultivar of winter squash that is round with smooth, slightly ribbed skin.
howToPlant: Needs lots of space. Plant in mounds after soil is warm. 100-120 sq ft per plant.
prevention: Use mulch, check leaf undersides for bug eggs, use neem oil.

name: Sweet Potato
info: A dicotyledonous plant that belongs to the bindweed or morning glory family.
howToPlant: Plant 'slips' (sprouted cuttings) in ridges of loose soil when weather is hot.
prevention: Use certified slips, rotate crops every 3-4 years, avoid manure overkill.

name: Corn
info: A tall cereal grass widely grown for its large ears of starchy grain.
howToPlant: Plant in blocks of at least 4 rows for wind pollination. Needs heavy nitrogen.
prevention: Remove smut galls before they burst, control flea beetles.

name: Okra
info: Known in many English-speaking countries as ladies' fingers or bhindi.
howToPlant: Requires hot weather. Soak seeds for 24h before planting. Harvest daily.
prevention: Rotate with grains, solarize soil, remove old crop residue.

name: Brussels Sprouts
info: A member of the Gemmifera Group of cabbages, grown for its edible buds.
howToPlant: Start early, needs a long season. Best harvested after frost.
prevention: Spray with strong water stream for aphids, use BT for worms.

name: Artichoke
info: The globe artichoke is a variety of a species of thistle cultivated as food.
howToPlant: Perennial in warm zones. Needs rich soil and lots of space.
prevention: Space plants well, remove dead leaves in winter.

name: Leek
info: A vegetable, a cultivar of Allium ampeloprasum, the broadleaf wild leek.
howToPlant: Mound soil around stems to blanch them white. Very hardy.
prevention: Avoid over-planting, use netting against thrips.

name: Turnip
info: A root vegetable commonly grown in temperate climates worldwide.
howToPlant: Sow in early spring or late summer. Use both roots and greens.
prevention: Keep soil moist to prevent splitting, use row covers.

name: Parsnip
info: A root vegetable related to the carrot and parsley.
howToPlant: Seeds take long to germinate. Needs cold weather to develop sweetness.
prevention: Ensure good drainage, don't use fresh manure.

name: Swiss Chard
info: A leafy green vegetable often grown for its colorful stems.
howToPlant: Similar to beets. Easy to grow and very productive all season.
prevention: Thin to allow airflow, remove yellowing leaves.

name: Bok Choy
info: A type of Chinese cabbage that does not form heads.
howToPlant: Prefers cool weather and rich, moist soil. Grows quickly.
prevention: Use row covers, avoid watering late in the day.

name: Arugula
info: Also known as rocket, a peppery, leafy green.
howToPlant: Sow in early spring. Tends to bolt in heat.
prevention: Plant in partial shade during heat, use row covers.

name: Fennel
info: A flowering plant species in the carrot family, with a bulbous base.
howToPlant: Prefers rich soil and consistent moisture. Don't plant near dill.
prevention: Avoid overwatering, use insecticidal soap for aphids.

name: Ginger
info: A flowering plant whose rhizome is widely used as a spice or folk medicine.
howToPlant: Plant rhizomes in warm, humid environment with indirect light.
prevention: Ensure excellent drainage, use healthy seed rhizomes.

name: Turmeric
info: A rhizomatous herbaceous perennial plant of the ginger family.
howToPlant: Similar to ginger. Needs a long, warm season and plenty of water.
prevention: Prevent waterlogging, treat rhizomes with fungicide if needed.

name: Chili (Green)
info: Varieties of pepper grown for their spicy heat.
howToPlant: Start indoors. Needs heat and sunny position.
prevention: Avoid overhead watering, use mulch, rotate crops.

name: Bitter Gourd
info: A tropical and subtropical vine grown for its edible fruit.
howToPlant: Provide a trellis. Needs hot climate; soak seeds before sowing.
prevention: Bag fruits, use pheromone traps, ensure drainage.

name: Bottle Gourd
info: A vine grown for its fruit, which can be eaten young or dried as a container.
howToPlant: Large vine; needs strong support. Warm soil essential.
prevention: Control aphids, use neem spray for mildew.

name: Ridge Gourd
info: A popular vegetable in Asia, part of the cucumber family.
howToPlant: Trailing vine. Sow seeds 1 inch deep in warm soil.
prevention: Remove infected parts, use light traps for borers.

name: Fenugreek
info: Used as a herb (dried or fresh leaves) and a spice (seeds).
howToPlant: Grows very fast. Sow seeds thick for greens.
prevention: Avoid overwatering, use well-drained soil.

name: Mustard Greens
info: The leaves of the mustard plant Brassica juncea.
howToPlant: Prefers cool weather. Sow seeds 1/4 inch deep.
prevention: Rotate crops, space well, water at base.

name: Kohlrabi
info: A biennial vegetable, a low, stout cultivar of wild cabbage.
howToPlant: Harvest when the 'bulb' is small (3 inches) for best flavor.
prevention: Similar to cabbage/broccoli care.

name: Watercress
info: A rapidly growing, aquatic or semi-aquatic, perennial plant.
howToPlant: Needs lots of running water or very wet soil. Prefers alkaline water.
prevention: Maintain water quality, use mesh to protect from beetles.

name: Scallion
info: Vegetables derived from various species in the genus Allium.
howToPlant: Sow seeds every 2 weeks for continuous harvest. Very easy.
prevention: Avoid over-fertilizing, use mulch.

name: Shallot
info: A botanical variety of the onion, forming clusters of small bulbs.
howToPlant: Plant individual bulbs in fall or early spring. Sweet and mild flavor.
prevention: Store in cool/dry place, ensure drainage.

name: Yam
info: Species of flowering plants in the family Dioscoreaceae.
howToPlant: Needs a long, hot season and deep, loose soil. Climbing vine.
prevention: Use virus-free tubers, rotate with non-host crops.

name: Taro
info: A tropical plant grown primarily for its edible corms.
howToPlant: Needs constant moisture and warm temperatures. Can grow in standing water.
prevention: Avoid planting infected suckers, remove old leaves.

name: Elephant Foot Yam
info: A tropical tuber crop grown in Asia and Africa.
howToPlant: Plant corm pieces in deep pits with organic matter.
prevention: Ensure no water stagnation, use healthy corms.

name: Cluster Beans
info: Known as Gavar, an annual legume grown in semi-arid regions.
howToPlant: Needs warm climate. Sow seeds 1 inch deep in clusters.
prevention: Use certified seeds, avoid overhead irrigation.

name: Apple
info: A popular pomaceous fruit grown on deciduous trees.
howToPlant: Plant grafted trees in early spring. Needs two varieties for pollination.
prevention: Prune for airflow, remove fallen leaves, use resistant varieties.

name: Banana
info: An elongated, edible fruit produced by several kinds of large herbaceous flowering plants.
howToPlant: Plant suckers in rich, moist soil. Needs high heat and humidity.
prevention: Remove infected leaves, improve drainage, use resistant suckers.

name: Mango
info: A tropical stone fruit known for its sweet, juicy pulp.
howToPlant: Plant in deep, well-drained soil in a sunny spot. Grafted trees are best.
prevention: Spray with copper fungicide during flowering, prune regularly.

name: Orange
info: A citrus fruit known for its high vitamin C content.
howToPlant: Plant in early spring in sandy, well-dramed soil. Needs full sun.
prevention: Use disease-free stock, control citrus psyllid.

name: Strawberry
info: A widely grown hybrid species of the genus Fragaria.
howToPlant: Plant runners in spring. Needs crown at soil level and lots of sun.
prevention: Mulch with straw, ensure drainage, rotate beds.

name: Grape
info: A fruit, botanically a berry, of the deciduous woody vines of the flowering plant genus Vitis.
howToPlant: Needs a strong trellis and heavy annual pruning. Full sun essential.
prevention: Maximize air circulation, prune carefully, use fungicides.

name: Pineapple
info: A tropical plant with an edible fruit and the most economically significant plant in the family Bromeliaceae.
howToPlant: Plant the crown from a store-bought pineapple in sandy soil.
prevention: Ensure drainage, control ants that tend to mealybugs.

name: Watermelon
info: A vine-like flowering plant originally from West Africa.
howToPlant: Needs hot weather and long growing season. Plant in mounds.
prevention: Avoid overhead watering, use mulch, rotate crops.

name: Papaya
info: A tropical fruit tree that grows very quickly.
howToPlant: Plant seeds directly. Needs lots of water but perfect drainage.
prevention: Control aphids, ensure drainage, use resistant seeds.

name: Pomegranate
info: A fruit-bearing deciduous shrub or small tree.
howToPlant: Very drought tolerant once established. Likes hot, dry summers.
prevention: Prune to open center, remove mummified fruit.

name: Lemon
info: A species of small evergreen tree in the flowering plant family Rutaceae.
howToPlant: Similar to orange. Sensitive to cold. Needs frequent feeding.
prevention: Do not overwater, use well-draining citrus mix.

name: Lime
info: A citrus fruit, which is typically round, green in color.
howToPlant: Similar to lemon but even more sensitive to frost.
prevention: Use neem oil for leaf miners, avoid trunk wetting.

name: Avocado
info: A tree likely originating from south-central Mexico.
howToPlant: Plant in loose, well-draining soil. Does not like wet feet.
prevention: Incorporate gypsum, ensure drainage, use certified stock.

name: Cherry
info: The fruit of many plants of the genus Prunus.
howToPlant: Needs winter chill. Well-drained, slightly acidic soil.
prevention: Clean up fallen fruit, prune for airflow.

name: Blueberry
info: Perennial flowering plants with blue or purple berries.
howToPlant: Needs highly acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5). Use peat moss.
prevention: Mulch heavily, remove blighted stems, maintain acidity.

name: Raspberry
info: The edible fruit of a multitude of plant species in the genus Rubus.
howToPlant: Plant in rows with support. Cut back old canes after harvest.
prevention: Remove wild brambles nearby, ensure drainage.

name: Blackberry
info: An edible fruit produced by many species in the genus Rubus.
howToPlant: Very vigorous, can be invasive. Provide sturdy support.
prevention: Remove infected plants immediately, prune for light.

name: Peach
info: A deciduous tree native to the region of Northwest China.
howToPlant: Short-lived but fast-growing. Needs heavy pruning annually.
prevention: Spray with lime sulfur in winter, thin fruits.

name: Plum
info: A fruit of some species in Prunus subg. Prunus.
howToPlant: Easier than peaches. Need two varieties for best pollination.
prevention: Prune out galls/knots, use certified trees.

name: Apricot
info: The fruit of the tree Prunus armeniaca.
howToPlant: Early blooming; avoid frost pockets. Needs well-drained soil.
prevention: Avoid wounding the bark, prune in dry weather.

name: Pear
info: Trees of the genus Pyrus in the family Rosaceae.
howToPlant: Easier to grow than apples in some climates. Long-lived.
prevention: Prune out blighted branches 12 inches below damage.

name: Guava
info: Tropical trees of the genus Psidium.
howToPlant: Tolerates various soils but prefers rich ones. Needs full sun.
prevention: Prune to maintain shape, avoid waterlogging.

name: Kiwi
info: The edible berry of several species of woody vines in the genus Actinidia.
howToPlant: Need male and female plants. Very heavy vine needs massive support.
prevention: Exceptional drainage, use clean pruning tools.

name: Dragon Fruit
info: The fruit of several different cactus species.
howToPlant: Plant cuttings in sandy soil. Needs a sturdy post for support.
prevention: Don't overwater, ensure airflow, use copper spray.

name: Lychee
info: The sole member of the genus Litchi in the soapberry family.
howToPlant: Needs tropical climate with cool, dry winters and hot, wet summers.
prevention: Sulphur spray for mites, avoid low-lying wet spots.

name: Jackfruit
info: The largest tree-borne fruit in the world.
howToPlant: Grows into a massive tree. Needs deep soil and tropical heat.
prevention: Bag young fruits, remove decaying matter from tree.

name: Custard Apple
info: A common name for several species of Annona.
howToPlant: Prefers hot, humid climate. Needs regular watering during fruit set.
prevention: Prune for light, use horticultural oils for bugs.

name: Sapodilla
info: A long-lived, evergreen tree native to southern Mexico.
howToPlant: Very wind resistant. Grows slowly. Drought tolerant.
prevention: Remove infested twigs, ensure good soil health.

name: Fig
info: A species of small tree in the mulberry family.
howToPlant: Very easy to grow. Can be grown in pots to restrict roots.
prevention: Avoid wetting leaves, prune dead wood.

name: Dates
info: A flowering plant species in the palm family, Arecaceae.
howToPlant: Requires desert conditions. Very long-lived.
prevention: Avoid planting in infested areas, maintain sanitation.

name: Coconut
info: The fruit of the coconut palm, a member of the family Arecaceae.
howToPlant: Plant the whole nut halfway in sandy soil. Needs salt/sea air.
prevention: Maintain tree health, use resistant cultivars.

name: Passion Fruit
info: A vine species of passion flower native to southern Brazil.
howToPlant: Fast-growing vine. Needs a fence. Short-lived vine (5 years).
prevention: Control aphids, provide nitrogen, rotate vines.

name: Star Fruit
info: Also known as Carambola, known for its star shape when sliced.
howToPlant: Needs full sun and lots of water. Tropical climate.
prevention: Spray with soapy water, ensure drainage.

name: Mulberry
info: Deciduous trees known for their sweet, blackberry-like fruits.
howToPlant: Very hardy and fast-growing. Can be messy; plant away from paths.
prevention: Remove infected berries, prune for airflow.

name: Cranberry
info: Evergreen dwarf shrubs or trailing vines.
howToPlant: Needs bog-like conditions; very acidic, wet soil.
prevention: Ensure good water circulation, sand every few years.

name: Gooseberry
info: A species of Ribes, native to Europe and western Asia.
howToPlant: Very hardy. Prefers cool, moist soil and partial shade in hot areas.
prevention: Choose resistant varieties, hand-pick larvae.

name: Persimmon
info: The edible fruit of a number of species of trees in the genus Diospyros.
howToPlant: Beautiful ornamental tree. Easy to grow, few pests.
prevention: Ensure drainage, prune out dead wood.

name: Quince
info: A small deciduous tree that bears a pome fruit.
howToPlant: Similar to pear. Beautiful flowers. Fruit must be cooked.
prevention: Similar to pear care (antiseptic pruning).

name: Nectarine
info: A cultivar of peach with smooth, fuzzless skin.
howToPlant: Same as peach. Very sensitive to 'peach leaf curl'.
prevention: Use fungicide spray in dormancy, thin heavily.

name: Cantaloupe
info: A variety of muskmelon with ribbed skin.
howToPlant: Needs hot soil and space. Plant in mounds.
prevention: Avoid overhead watering, use mulch.

name: Honeydew Melon
info: A smooth-skinned variety of muskmelon.
howToPlant: Similar to cantaloupe but takes slightly longer to ripen.
prevention: Control insects, ensure consistent water.

name: Rambutan
info: A medium-sized tropical tree in the soapberry family.
howToPlant: Purely tropical; won't survive cold. Needs humid air.
prevention: Maintain humidity but ensure airflow, copper sprays.

name: Mangosteen
info: A tropical evergreen tree believed to have originated in SE Asia.
howToPlant: Extremely difficult to grow out of native climate. Slow growing.
prevention: Maintain perfect drainage, protect from wind.

name: Durian
info: The 'King of Fruits', known for its large size and strong odor.
howToPlant: Needs equatorial climate and deep, fertile soil.
prevention: Avoid wounding trunk, improve drainage.

name: Pomelo
info: The largest citrus fruit, ancestor of the grapefruit.
howToPlant: Hardier than some citrus. Needs lots of sun.
prevention: Same as orange/lemon care.

name: Tangerine
info: A group of orange-colored citrus fruit involving hybrids of mandarin.
howToPlant: Easy to grow in sunny spots. Good for pots.
prevention: Oil sprays for mites, prune for light.

name: Clementine
info: A tangor, a citrus fruit hybrid between a willowleaf mandarin orange and a sweet orange.
howToPlant: Similar to tangerine. Very sweet and seedless.
prevention: Ensure drainage, use light traps.

name: Elderberry
info: Small black or blue-black berries used in jellies and wine.
howToPlant: Grows in many soil types. Useful for hedgerows.
prevention: Prune out old canes, leave space between plants.

name: Currant
info: Small berries that grow on deciduous shrubs (red, white, or black).
howToPlant: Loves cool, northern climates. Prefers moist soil.
prevention: Don't plant near white pines, use resistant types.

name: Jambolan
info: Also known as Black Plum or Jamun, an evergreen tropical tree.
howToPlant: Very hardy once established. Fast growing.
prevention: Regular pruning, bag fruits if needed.

name: Rice
info: Rice is the primary staple food for more than half of the world's population. It is a moisture-loving crop typically grown in flooded paddies.
howToPlant: Transplant 18-22 day old seedlings at 15x10cm spacing. Maintain 5cm water level. Treat seeds with Azospirillum. Apply zinc sulphate 0.5% if deficient
diseases: Herbicides: Propanil 2kg/ha at 20 DAS. Insecticides: Carbofuran 3CG 33kg/ha for stem borer. Fungicides: Carbendazim for blast
prevention: N:120 kg/ha, P2O5:60 kg/ha, K2O:40 kg/ha. Apply FYM 12.5 t/ha. Split N in 3 doses: 50% basal, 25% tillering, 25% panicle initiation. Full P&K as basal

name: Chickpea (Gram)
info: A high-protein legume crop cultivated as a Rabi grain, crucial for soil nitrogen fixation.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 30-70 kg/ha based on seed size. Treat with Rhizobium 200g/8kg + PSB. One irrigation at pod initiation. Sow in October
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g + Carbendazim 1g/kg. Insecticides: Endosulfan 0.07%, NPV 250 LE/ha for pod borer. Spray 5% NSKE for IPM
prevention: N:20-30 kg/ha, P2O5:40-60 kg/ha, K2O:17-30 kg/ha. Apply FYM 5 t/ha. All as basal dose. Foliar spray of 2% urea at flowering

name: Kidney Beans (Rajma)
info: A popular pulse crop widely used in North Indian cuisine, rich in protein and fiber.
howToPlant: Spacing: Bush type 45x10cm, Vine type 60x15cm. Seed depth: 3-4cm. Plant after last frost. Provide trellis for vine varieties. Water regularly but avoid waterlogging
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram or Captan 3g/kg. Insecticides: Imidacloprid for aphids. Use organic neem oil. Avoid chemical overuse
prevention: N:20-25 kg/ha, P2O5:40-50 kg/ha, K2O:20-30 kg/ha. Avoid excessive N. Apply balanced 5-10-10 NPK at planting. Beans fix their own nitrogen

name: Pigeon Peas (Tur/Arhar)
info: A major source of protein in vegetarian diets, highly drought-tolerant legume crop.
howToPlant: Spacing: 60x20cm. Seed rate: 15-20 kg/ha. Inoculate with Rhizobium + PSB. Irrigate at branching (30 DAS), flowering (70 DAS), podding (110 DAS)
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g + Carbendazim 1g/kg + Rhizobium. Herbicides: Pendimethalin 0.75-1 kg/ha pre-emergence. Insecticides for pod borer
prevention: N:25-30 kg/ha, P2O5:40-50 kg/ha, K2O:30 kg/ha as basal. Apply FYM 5-10 t/ha. Zinc 3 kg/ha, Sulphur 20 kg/ha in deficient soils

name: Moth Beans
info: An exceptionally drought-resistant legume crop grown in sandy soils, popular in Western India.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 12-15 kg/ha. Drought tolerant. Grows well in arid regions. Light irrigation at flowering improves yield
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g/kg. Insecticides: Quinalphos or Monocrotophos for pod borers. Herbicides: Pendimethalin pre-emergence
prevention: N:15-20 kg/ha, P2O5:30-40 kg/ha, K2O:15-20 kg/ha. Apply FYM 4-5 t/ha. All as basal application. Minimal nitrogen needed due to N-fixation

name: Mung Bean (Green Gram/Moong)
info: A quick-growing legume harvested for its green seeds, widely consumed as sprouts and dals.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 15-20 kg/ha. Seed treatment with FIR method (Fungicide-Insecticide-Rhizobium). Light irrigation at germination, flowering, pod formation
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 3g/kg + Rhizobium culture. Herbicides: Pendimethalin 30% at sowing. Insecticides: Imidacloprid for thrips and aphids
prevention: N:15-20 kg/ha, P2O5:30-40 kg/ha, K2O:20 kg/ha. Apply SSP 100 kg/acre + Zinc Sulphate 4-6 kg/acre at sowing. Excess N not required

name: Black Gram (Urad)
info: A major pulse crop native to India, highly valued for its sticky texture, ideal for making idli and dosa batters.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 15-20 kg/ha. Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium + PSB culture. One or two irrigations at flowering and pod development
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2.5g/kg + Rhizobium. Insecticides: Quinalphos for pod borer. Fungicides: Carbendazim for wilt and leaf spot
prevention: N:20-25 kg/ha, P2O5:40-50 kg/ha, K2O:20-25 kg/ha. Apply FYM 5 t/ha. All fertilizers as basal. Rhizobium inoculation reduces N need

name: Lentil (Masoor)
info: An ancient brushy legume crop known for its lens-shaped seeds, quick cooking and high in nutrients.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 30-40 kg/ha. Inoculate with Rhizobium leguminosarum. Avoid waterlogging. One irrigation at flowering if needed
diseases: Seed treatment: Carbendazim 2.5g/kg. Herbicides: Pendimethalin 0.75-1 kg/ha pre-emergence. Insecticides for aphids and pod borer
prevention: N:15-20 kg/ha, P2O5:40-50 kg/ha, K2O:20 kg/ha. Apply FYM 1-2 t/ha. Sulphur 8 kg/acre. Zinc foliar spray 2% if deficient

name: Muskmelon
info: A sweet, juicy summer melon with ribbed skin and orange flesh, rich in vitamin A.
howToPlant: Spacing: 2x1.5m or 1.5x0.6m. Seed rate: 2-2.5 kg/ha. Pinching of vine tips encourages branching. Irrigate at flowering and fruiting. Mulching beneficial
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g/kg. Insecticides: Imidacloprid for aphids. Fungicides: Mancozeb for downy mildew. Herbicides: Pendimethalin pre-emergence
prevention: N:50-60 kg/ha, P:40-50 kg/ha, K:50-60 kg/ha. Apply FYM 15-20 t/ha. Split application: basal and at vine growth stage

name: Cotton
info: A soft, fluffy staple fiber that grows in a boll, used worldwide for textile manufacturing.
howToPlant: Spacing: 60-90x30-45cm. Seed rate: 10-12 kg/ha. Treat seeds with fungicide + insecticide + micronutrients. Irrigate at critical stages. Gap filling within 10 days
diseases: Seed treatment: Imidacloprid 5ml/kg. Insecticides: Profenofos for bollworms, Acetamiprid for sucking pests. Fungicides: Carbendazim for wilt
prevention: N:120-150 kg/ha, P:60-75 kg/ha, K:60-75 kg/ha. Basal: 50kg DAP + 25kg MOP. Split N application: 30% basal, 30% at 30-35 DAS, 40% at 60-65 DAS

name: Jute
info: A long, soft, shiny vegetable fiber spun into coarse, strong threads, crucial for eco-friendly packaging.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x7-10cm (line sowing). Seed rate: 5-6 kg/ha. Requires warm humid climate, 5-8cm weekly rainfall. Harvest when 50% plants flower. Retting takes 15-20 days
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2.5g/kg. Insecticides: Quinalphos for hairy caterpillars. Spray 2% urea at 40-45 and 70-75 DAS in drought conditions
prevention: N:60 kg/ha (20 basal + 10 at 20-25 DAS + 10 at 35-40 DAS), P:20 kg/ha, K:20 kg/ha. Apply FYM 5 t/ha at last ploughing

name: Coffee
info: A globally beloved beverage crop grown on evergreen shrubs, preferring high altitude and shade.
howToPlant: Spacing: varies by region. Requires 1500-2500mm rainfall. Shade management essential. Mulching with coffee husk/leaves. Remove suckers. Prune after harvest
diseases: Fungicides: Bordeaux mixture 1% for leaf rust. Insecticides: Quinalphos for white stem borer. Use Trichoderma as biocontrol agent
prevention: Bearing plants: N:140-300g, P:55g, K:270g per vine/year. Apply in 2 splits (June & Sept). Include micronutrients Zn, B, Mo. Organic: 10kg FYM + vermicompost

name: Barley
info: A major cereal grain used in bread, beverages, and animal feed. Highly adaptable to poor soils.
howToPlant: Spacing: 22.5cm row spacing. Seed rate: 100 kg/ha. Sow at optimum time. Irrigate at CRI, tillering, jointing, flowering, milk stage. Light soils preferred
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram or Vitavax 2.5g/kg. Herbicides: Isoproturon for weed control. Fungicides: Propiconazole for rusts
prevention: N:60-80 kg/ha, P:40-50 kg/ha, K:30-40 kg/ha. Apply FYM 10 t/ha. Half N + full P,K as basal. Remaining N at tillering and flag leaf stage

name: Sunflower
info: A tall annual crop grown for its oil-rich seeds and cheerful large yellow flower heads.
howToPlant: Spacing: 60x30cm. Seed rate: 8-10 kg/ha. Sow at 4-5cm depth. Critical irrigation stages: flowering and seed filling. Drain excess water. Harvest at physiological maturity
diseases: Seed treatment: Imidacloprid 5ml/kg. Insecticides: Chlorpyriphos for head borer. Fungicides: Mancozeb for rust, Ridomil for downy mildew
prevention: N:60-80 kg/ha, P:60-80 kg/ha, K:40-60 kg/ha. Apply FYM 12-15 t/ha. Half N + full P,K basal. Remaining N at 30 DAS. Foliar borax 0.2% at flowering

name: Soybean (Soyabean)
info: An extremely versatile oilseed legume, providing high-quality protein and oil for food and industry.
howToPlant: Spacing: 45x5cm. Seed rate: 70-80 kg/ha. Inoculate with Rhizobium + PSB. Two-three irrigations at critical stages. Harvest when 95% pods mature, leaves drop
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g + Carbendazim 1g/kg + Rhizobium. Herbicides: Pendimethalin 1kg/ha. Insecticides: Quinalphos for girdle beetle and leaf eater
prevention: N:20-30 kg/ha, P:60-80 kg/ha, K:30-40 kg/ha, S:20-30 kg/ha. Apply FYM 5 t/ha. All as basal. Rhizobium fixes 60-80% N requirement

name: Jowar (Sorghum)
info: A highly drought-resistant millet crop, providing staple grain and fodder in dry regions.
howToPlant: Spacing: 45x15cm. Seed rate: 10-12 kg/ha. Thin to 2 plants/hill. Irrigate at tillering, flowering, grain filling. Bird protection needed during maturity
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2.5g/kg. Insecticides: Chlorpyriphos for shoot fly, stem borer. Malathion for aphids. Systemic insecticides at boot stage
prevention: N:80-100 kg/ha, P:40-50 kg/ha, K:40 kg/ha. Apply FYM 10 t/ha. Half N + full P,K basal. Remaining N at 30-35 DAS

name: Ragi (Finger Millet)
info: A calcium-rich coarse grain millet crop, highly sustainable and resilient to harsh conditions.
howToPlant: Spacing: 22.5x10cm. Seed rate: 10 kg/ha (transplanting), 40 kg/ha (direct seeding). Transplant 25-30 day seedlings. Irrigate at critical stages. Nutritious millet crop
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2.5g/kg. Insecticides: Chlorpyriphos for shoot fly. Fungicides: Carbendazim for blast disease
prevention: N:50-75 kg/ha, P:40-50 kg/ha, K:25-37.5 kg/ha. Apply FYM 7.5 t/ha. Half N + full P&K basal. Remaining N at 30 DAS

name: Rapeseed (Sarson)
info: A bright yellow flowering member of the mustard family, grown widely for oilseed production.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 4-5 kg/ha. Sow at optimum time for avoiding aphids. Irrigate at flowering and pod filling. Harvest when pods turn brown
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g/kg. Insecticides: Quinalphos for aphids, Chlorpyriphos for sawfly. Fungicides: Mancozeb for Alternaria blight
prevention: N:80-120 kg/ha, P:40-60 kg/ha, K:40 kg/ha, S:40 kg/ha. Apply FYM 10 t/ha. Half N + full P,K,S basal. Remaining N at 30-35 DAS and flowering

name: Wheat
info: One of the most critical cereal grains worldwide, forming the basis of flour, bread, and pastries.
howToPlant: Spacing: 20-23cm row spacing. Seed rate: 100-125 kg/ha. Sow in Nov for timely, Dec for late sowing. Irrigate at CRI, tillering, jointing, flowering, milking, dough stages
diseases: Seed treatment: Vitavax 2.5g/kg. Herbicides: 2,4-D for broadleaf weeds, Isoproturon for grassy weeds. Fungicides: Propiconazole for rusts
prevention: N:120-150 kg/ha, P:60 kg/ha, K:40 kg/ha. Apply FYM 10 t/ha. Full P&K + 1/3 N basal. 1/3 N at CRI, 1/3 at tillering/jointing

name: Horse Gram
info: A rustic legume crop known for its medicinal benefits, high protein, and ability to thrive on poor soils.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 25-30 kg/ha. Drought tolerant crop. Suitable for poor soils. One or two irrigations if available. Harvest when pods mature
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2g/kg + Rhizobium. Insecticides: Chlorpyriphos for pod borer. Spray neem oil for eco-friendly pest control
prevention: N:25 kg/ha, P:50 kg/ha, K:12.5 kg/ha. Apply FYM 7.5 t/ha. All as basal. Being a legume, needs minimal N

name: Coriander (Dhania)
info: An aromatic herb whose leaves are used fresh, and seeds are dried and ground as a primary spice.
howToPlant: Spacing: 30x10cm. Seed rate: 15-20 kg/ha. Soak seeds 24 hrs before sowing. Light irrigation at 10-15 day intervals. Harvest when seeds turn brown
diseases: Seed treatment: Thiram 2.5g/kg. Insecticides: Malathion for aphids. Fungicides: Mancozeb for powdery mildew and wilt
prevention: N:40-60 kg/ha, P:30-40 kg/ha, K:20-30 kg/ha. Apply FYM 10-15 t/ha. Half N + full P&K basal. Remaining N at 30 DAS

name: Cardamom
info: Known as the 'Queen of Spices', a premium highly aromatic spice grown in shade and humid rainforests.
howToPlant: Spacing: 2x2m or 2x1m (high density). Plant during monsoon. Requires shade (50%). Mulching essential. Remove dried leaves. Harvest capsules at right maturity
diseases: Insecticides: Quinalphos for thrips and shoot fly. Fungicides: Bordeaux mixture for leaf spot. Use biocontrol agents like Trichoderma
prevention: N:75-120 kg/ha, P:75-120 kg/ha, K:150-240 kg/ha based on spacing. Apply FYM + Neem cake 2.9 t/ha. Apply in May-June and Sept-Oct

name: Black Pepper
info: Known as the 'King of Spices', a woody climbing vine yielding small pungent berries that are dried.
howToPlant: Spacing: 2-3m. Plant as cuttings near support trees. Requires 2000-3000mm rainfall. Proper drainage essential. Mulching important. Apply micronutrients (Zn, B, Mo)
diseases: Fungicides: Bordeaux mixture 1% for foot rot and diseases. Use Trichoderma 500g/vine/year as biocontrol. Spray Neem oil 0.5% for pests
prevention: N:50g, P:50g, K:150g per vine (young). Adult: 140g N, 55g P, 270g K per vine/year. Apply 10kg FYM + 500g Neem cake + Ash. Apply in May-June & Sept-Oct

name: Drumstick (Moringa)
info: A fast-growing multi-purpose tree yielding highly nutritious pods (drumsticks), leaves, and seeds.
howToPlant: Spacing: 2.5x2.5m. Plant seeds/cuttings. Annual variety: PKM 1, PKM 2 (60-90cm height, harvest after 90 days). Prune to maintain height. Multiple harvests possible
diseases: Insecticides: Quinalphos for pod fly and caterpillars. Fungicides: Bordeaux mixture for leaf spot diseases. Relatively pest-resistant crop
prevention: Young plants: 50-100g N, 50g P, 50g K. Bearing trees: 200-300g N, 100-150g P, 200-250g K + 10-15kg FYM per tree. Apply in 2-3 splits
